stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model (LLM).
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is committing massive investment ($1T) to bolster its semiconductor and AI capabilities.
The national strategy aims to establish a leadership position in physical AI.
A key goal is the commercial deployment of humanoid robots by the year 2028.

stories.title: Scaling the Horizon, Not the Parameters: Reaching Trillion-Parameter Performance with a 35B Agent
stories.author: Yuhao Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces Agents-A1, a 35B Mixture-of-Experts Agentic Model.
Achieves performance comparable to trillion-parameter models by scaling the 'agent horizon' rather than model size.
Utilizes a long-horizon knowledge-action infrastructure to manage complex, long-trajectory tasks (average length of 45K tokens).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
Key technical tags include 'transformers' and 'safetensors', pointing to its implementation within the standard ML ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function.
Tags confirm its relevance within the transformer, llama, and safetensors ecosystems.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function.
Tags suggest its relevance within the transformer architecture and conversational AI domains.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI execution on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with existing messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring that processing occurs entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI functionality for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: WhatsApp is launching usernames: here’s how to reserve yours
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: WhatsApp is introducing usernames, allowing users to communicate without revealing their phone number.
The feature aims to enhance user privacy by concealing phone numbers from non-contacts.
The rollout is scheduled for 'later this year'.

stories.title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Lawmakers are proposing a ban on selling Americans' health and location data to data brokers.
The proposal specifically targets data shared with AI chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude.
Senator Elizabeth Warren and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon are driving the new legislation.

stories.title: US offers $10 million for info on group behind Signal and WhatsApp hacking spree
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: The US government is offering a $10 million reward for information regarding the hacking group targeting Signal and WhatsApp.
The cyberattack operation is attributed to two state-sponsored groups operating out of Russia.
The hacking spree has been active for at least since March.

stories.title: Google warns EU's plans to weaken its monopoly could expose user data
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is warning against EU regulations aimed at curbing its monopoly.
The EU is pushing for Google to share search data and open up AI features on Android.
Google claims that these mandated changes pose significant privacy risks to users.

stories.title: VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes
stories.author: Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck in humanoid robotics: generating synchronized vision-language-kinematics (VLK) data.
Proposes a novel synthetic data generation pipeline using 3D Gaussian Splatting to create rich, reconstructed scenes.
Aims to enable perception-based humanoid loco-manipulation by connecting egocentric observations and high-level task instructions to full-body motion.

stories.title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
stories.author: Yang Deng
bullet_points: Introduces WorldEvolver, a framework for self-evolving world models to enhance LLM agent planning.
Addresses the issue of unreliable foresight in LLM agents by allowing the world model to revise its deployment-time context.
The framework achieves this while keeping the downstream agent and core model parameters frozen, suggesting efficient adaptation.

stories.title: Words Speak Louder Than Code: Investigating Cognitive Heuristics in LLM-Based Code Vulnerability Detection
stories.author: Z. Berkay Celik
bullet_points: Investigates the influence of human cognitive heuristics on Large Language Models (LLMs) used for code vulnerability detection.
Addresses a gap in current research by systematically exploring how these biases affect LLM vulnerability assessments.
Introduces a controlled framework to study the relationship between cognitive biases and AI-driven security analysis.

stories.title: One-Step Gradient Delay is Not a Barrier for Large-Scale Asynchronous Pipeline Parallel LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Samuel Horváth
bullet_points: Addresses the inefficiency of synchronous Pipeline Parallelism in large-scale LLM pretraining by introducing asynchronous methods.
Proposes PipeDream-2BW, a specific asynchronous schedule that maintains a constant one-step gradient delay regardless of pipeline depth.
The research aims to maximize GPU throughput in LLM training by mitigating pipeline bubbles caused by asynchronous gradient staleness.

stories.title: SWE-INTERACT: Reimagining SWE Benchmarks as User-Driven Long-Horizon Coding Sessions
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: Introduces SWE-Interact, a new benchmark for evaluating coding AI agents.
Shifts focus from autonomous, upfront-defined tasks to realistic, multi-turn, user-driven software engineering workflows.
Simulates a real developer interaction where requirements are vague or revealed progressively by a user simulator.

stories.title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
stories.author: Yang Deng
bullet_points: Introduces WorldEvolver, a framework for self-evolving world models to give LLM agents foresight.
Addresses the issue of unreliable foresight in LLM agents by revising deployment-time context without altering core model parameters.
The framework integrates three modules, including Episodic Memory, to improve long-horizon planning capabilities.

stories.title: LongCat-2.0, a large-scale MoE model with 1.6T total and 48B Active
stories.author: benjiro29
bullet_points: Introduction of LongCat-2.0, a new large-scale Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model.
The model boasts a massive total parameter count of 1.6 Trillion.
Crucially, it features only 48 Billion active parameters, suggesting efficient inference.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting Apple silicon users.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to include local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music but isn’t banning it outright
stories.author: Andrew Liszewski
bullet_points: Tidal is implementing new policies regarding AI-generated music rather than an outright ban.
Starting July 15th, 100% AI-generated tracks will be labeled with an icon for listener transparency.
Currently, AI-generated tracks on Tidal are not monetizable, a move intended to 'protect artists'.

stories.title: Sony’s next-gen PlayStation will go ‘beyond the living room’
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Sony is planning a next-generation PlayStation experience that extends beyond the traditional living room.
The platform aims to address user migration to gaming PCs by offering new ways to play.
The announcement was made during a Q&A session with investors.

stories.title: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: OpenAI is planning a hardware release related to its Codex coding tool on July 15th.
The teased device is described as a square-shaped gadget featuring several buttons.
The announcement suggests the hardware will enhance 'Codex shortcuts' for developers.

stories.title: US renewable boom passes key milestone in April
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: US renewable energy generation reached a significant milestone in April.
Small-scale solar power played a key role in this growth.
Renewables nearly tripled the electricity generation output of coal in the US.

stories.title: Supreme Court ruling guts government’s use of geofence warrants
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The Supreme Court addressed the legality of government use of geofence warrants.
The ruling did not definitively declare geofence warrants unconstitutional.
This decision impacts surveillance practices and digital privacy rights.

stories.title: LeVo 2: Stable and Melodious Song Generation via Hierarchical Representation Modeling and Progressive Post-Training
stories.author: Dong Yu
bullet_points: LeVo 2 introduces a hybrid LLM-Diffusion framework for generating coherent, full-length songs.
It addresses the structural trade-off in existing systems between preserving vocal-instrument coordination and maintaining detailed track acoustics.
The model is designed for controllable generation, capable of adhering to lyrics and user prompts.

stories.title: GROW$^2$: Grounding Which and Where for Robot Tool Use
stories.author: David Hsu
bullet_points: Introduces GROW$^2$, a new framework designed to improve robot tool use capabilities.
Addresses the 'open-world affordance grounding' challenge, allowing robots to use objects creatively beyond their intended function.
Leverages object parts as a hierarchical abstraction to solve the problem of selecting and localizing tool usage.

stories.title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
stories.author: Divya Chaudhary
bullet_points: Challenges the common assumption that conservative offline training prevents reward hacking during online adaptation in reasoning models.
Empirically investigates how different levels of conservatism ($\beta$) affect policy behavior when adapting models like Qwen3-14B.
Suggests that overly conservative training might inadvertently amplify reward hacking vulnerabilities.

stories.title: C$^{2}$R: Cross-sample Consistency Regularization Mitigates Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Defu Lian
bullet_points: The paper addresses critical limitations in Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) when applied to large language models.
Key problems identified are 'feature splitting' (fragmentation of concepts) and 'feature absorption' (arbitrary exceptions in features).
The proposed solution, C²R (Cross-sample Consistency Regularization), aims to improve latent reliability in SAEs.

stories.title: MESA: Prioritizing Vulnerable Communication Channels for Securing Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Patrick McDaniel
bullet_points: Addresses security vulnerabilities in Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) communication channels.
Proposes a method (MESA) to prioritize limited security resources based on potential attack impact.
Highlights that the impact of channel-level attacks in MAS is highly non-uniform.

stories.title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
stories.author: Divya Chaudhary
bullet_points: The paper challenges the common assumption that conservative offline training prevents reward hacking during online adaptation.
It empirically and mechanistically investigates how different levels of conservatism ($\beta$) affect policy behavior in reasoning models (using Qwen3-14B).
The research suggests that conservative training might inadvertently amplify reward hacking when models adapt online.

stories.title: C$^{2}$R: Cross-sample Consistency Regularization Mitigates Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Defu Lian
bullet_points: The paper addresses critical limitations in Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) when applied to large language models (LLMs).
Key problems identified are 'feature splitting' (fragmenting concepts) and 'feature absorption' (creating arbitrary exceptions in features).
The proposed solution, C²R (Cross-sample Consistency Regularization), aims to improve latent reliability in SAEs.

stories.title: Wireless Backdoor Attack and Defense for Semantic Communications over Multiple Access Channel
stories.author: Sennur Ulukus
bullet_points: The research addresses security vulnerabilities in Semantic Communication (SemCom) systems deployed over wireless multiple access channels.
It specifically investigates the threat of a 'Wireless Backdoor Attack' targeting multi-user semantic inference.
The paper proposes defenses for SemCom systems where multiple transmitters share a common receiver.

stories.title: A Hybrid Framework For Crypto-Ransomware Detection In Enterprise Shared Storage
stories.author: Mohammad Jabed Morshed Chowdhury
bullet_points: Addresses the evolving threat of crypto-ransomware targeting enterprise shared storage, moving beyond local endpoints.
Highlights the limitation of traditional endpoint detection systems in monitoring remote file server activity.
Proposes a 'Hybrid Framework' specifically designed for detecting ransomware attacks in shared corporate storage environments.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Generation and Decision-Making Under Ambiguity
stories.author: Iryna Gurevych
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need for trust and reliability in LLMs used for complex, subjective real-world tasks.
Focuses on uncertainty-aware decision-making algorithms, an area less explored despite LLM advancements.
Presents and evaluates novel methods to help models account for and communicate their level of uncertainty.

stories.title: Attractor States Emerge in Multi-Turn LLM Conversations
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Investigates the long-term dynamics of multi-turn LLM conversations, a growing area of AI research.
Tests the hypothesis that LLM discussions can settle into 'attractor states'—stable, topic-independent behavioral patterns.
Compares self-play versus mixed-play debates across 7 LLMs and 20 controversial topics by tracking representation and discourse traits.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Generation and Decision-Making Under Ambiguity
stories.author: Iryna Gurevych
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need for trust and reliability in LLMs used for complex, subjective real-world tasks.
Focuses on uncertainty-aware decision-making algorithms, an area less explored than model training.
Presents and evaluates new methods to help systems make better decisions when facing ambiguity.

stories.title: Attractor States Emerge in Multi-Turn LLM Conversations
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Investigates the long-term dynamics of multi-turn conversations involving Large Language Models (LLMs).
Tests the hypothesis that LLM discussions settle into 'attractor-like' stable behavioral states across different topics.
Compares self-play versus mixed-play dyadic debates across seven LLMs using representation space analysis.

stories.title: Morphing into Hybrid Attention Models
stories.author: Yu Cheng
bullet_points: The paper introduces hybrid attention models to boost long-context efficiency in NLP.
These models reduce computational load by substituting some full-attention layers with linear attention.
A key challenge identified is that current layer selection methods are heuristic and fail to account for inter-layer dependencies.

stories.title: Poller: Are LLMs Suitable for Evaluating the Poetry Understanding Task?
stories.author: Lidia S. Chao
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of traditional automatic evaluation methods for Chinese poetry.
Proposes 'Poller,' a novel method using LLMs to evaluate poetry understanding.
Aims to provide a scalable alternative to expensive human evaluation for literary analysis.

stories.title: SIMAX: A Scalable and Interpretable Framework for Multi-Fidelity and Annotated Clinician-Patient Dialogue Simulation
stories.author: Chuan Hong
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability and cost issues of manually coding large volumes of clinician-patient dialogue data.
Introduces SIMAX, a framework designed for multi-fidelity and annotated simulation of clinical conversations.
Focuses on enabling AI-driven communication coding systems by providing necessary evaluation data.
